agenda_pressure: Keep □, its positive iterates, and orbit dynamics central rather than returning to local ⊠⊤ packaging.
Prefer exact equivalences and reusable criteria over one-off existential separators.
Reward unification of one-step and composite dynamics instead of proliferating parallel fixed-k variants.
Use counterexamples to isolate the roles of consistency and C5, not to motivate cosmetic repairs.
current_bottlenecks: No exact theorem yet identifies box^k fixed-point existence with ordinary Henkin existence for all positive k.
Composite orbit criteria still stop short of a full per-k equivalence with Henkin existence.
The consistent separator boundary below C5 remains constrained by orbit obstructions but not decided.
Too many queue items are still dominated by C5 canonicity or constant-reflection packaging.
desired_summary_changes: Collapse all positive-iterate box^k fixed points to ordinary Henkin existence.
Upgrade one-step orbit-repeat characterizations to exact per-k composite-dynamics criteria.
Settle whether consistent Gödel existence can persist without Henkin existence below C5.
Clarify whether consistency alone links Gödel existence to the top-box equation below C5.
important_verified_counterexamples: In equality-preorder `ACR` models with monotone `□`, determine whether the existence of a constant-reflection `APS` is equivalent to the existence of some `c` with `c = □c`.

Characterize exactly when a constant reflection operator can extend a given `ACR` and `Prov` structure to an `APS`: for fixed `c`, give necessary and sufficient conditions for the existence of `Reft` and `APS` with `∀ x, ⊠x = c`.
∀ {α : Type*} [ACR α] [ACR.Prov α] [ACR.Reft α] [ACR.APS α], ACR.Consistent α → Nonempty (ACR.GödelFixpoint α) → (⊠⊠(⊤ : α) ≐ ⊠⊤) → Nonempty (ACR.HenkinFixpoint α)
missing_bridges: A theorem collapsing arbitrary positive-iterate box^k fixed points to ordinary Henkin points.
A per-k orbit-repeat criterion exactly equivalent to Henkin existence.
A clean below-C5 consistency-sensitive bridge between Gödel existence and the top-box equation.
A sharp account of whether the known infinitary orbit obstructions are merely necessary or actually realized by consistent separators.

next_direction.guidance: Prioritize exact theorems showing that positive-iterate box^k fixed-point and orbit-repeat phenomena collapse to ordinary Henkin existence for every k > 0.
next_direction.label: positive_iterate_collapse
next_direction.rationale: This is the clearest summary-changing gap left after the verified one-step orbit characterization, and existing counterexamples already push C5 packaging and constant-reflection variants off the critical path while separator evidence shows that any failure case must evade finite or repeating dynamics.

overexplored_patterns: C5 witness-packaging and canonical-representative variants centered on ⊠⊤.
Constant-reflection refinements that only restate monotonicity-plus-bounds classifications.
Existential separator statements padded with extra infinitary conjuncts.
Degenerate-model classifications that do not feed back into composite collapse or separator structure.

canonical_targets: A characterization of fixed-point existence and uniqueness
soft_constraints: Keep `□`, `⊠`, and their composites visibly central
Prefer structural results over isolated examples
State clearly which phenomena depend on finiteness or infiniteness
Distinguish essential assumptions from technical conveniences
themes: Existence and uniqueness of fixed points for composites `△`
Counterexample models for nonexistence of fixed points
valued_problem_types: Sharp separation between finite and infinite underlying sets
Results stated for genuinely general classes of `△`
Logically meaningful phenomena even in restricted settings

theory_snapshot: The current theory has a solid one-step dynamical core: ordinary Henkin existence is exactly captured by repetition modulo equivalence along an inflationary □-orbit, and finite orbit behavior already forces Henkin points. The main open structural gap is whether this one-step picture collapses all positive iterates as well, both at the level of box^k fixed points and at the level of per-k orbit criteria. A second frontier is the consistent below-C5 boundary relating Gödel existence, Henkin failure, and the top-box equation. Verified counterexamples already block broad canonicity and constant-reflection repairs, so genuine progress must come from exact collapse theorems or sharp separator principles rather than more packaging variants.
